text:
The vaults beneath the keep are not crypts. They are archives. The walls are lined with shelves of frozen flesh — not decomposing, but preserved, each body tagged with a label in that same undecipherable cold-language. They are sorted by something. Size, perhaps. Or importance. Or something else entirely.

At the centre of the archive, a figure works. Pale and thin, wearing robes of stitched white fur, it moves between the shelves with the quiet efficiency of a librarian. It does not acknowledge you until you are close enough that fleeing would be pointless.

When it finally turns, you see its face is a mask of frost — behind it, two hollow eye sockets where flame once burned, long since extinguished. It opens its mouth and breathes out a cloud of cold that carries, buried in it, the distinct shape of a scream.
lore: The Pale Wraiths are what remain of the Coldfire priests after their god answered their prayers completely. They are not undead — they are post-living. The distinction matters to them, though it offers you no comfort.
